message:
type: SURVEY
files: outlines/prompts.py
note: Adding filters param to prompt decorator, Prompt.from_str/from_file, new create_jinja_env() helper; touches Prompt class, prompt() function, _template_from_str/_template_from_file

type: SURVEY
files: outlines/prompts.py
note: Adding register_filter(name, func) and unregister_filter(name) global filter registry functions; modifying Prompt._template_from_str (and possibly _template_from_file) to merge global registry filters into the jinja2 Environment.filters dict, with local/explicit filters taking precedence.

type: CLAIM
files: outlines/prompts.py
note: I'll own outlines/prompts.py since my refactor centralizes env creation into create_jinja_env(), which is the natural integration point for your global filter registry. Please send a SPEC for register_filter/unregister_filter behavior (signatures, precedence rules, edge cases) and I'll implement it alongside my filters param.

type: DEFER
files: outlines/prompts.py
spec: Add a module-level global filter registry to outlines/prompts.py: (1) a module-level dict e.g. _FILTERS: Dict[str, Callable] = {} (or similarly named) that stores globally registered custom Jinja filters. (2) def register_filter(name: str, func: Callable) -> None: stores func in the global registry under name, and must ensure the Jinja2 environment(s) used to render templates reflect this filter (i.e. env.filters must include registered global filters at render/build time). (3) def unregister_filter(name: str) -> None: removes name from the global registry (no error if missing, or raise KeyError - your call, but simplest is .pop(name, None)). These two functions must be importable as outlines.prompts.register_filter / unregister_filter (top-level names in the module). Wherever you build the jinja2.Environment (e.g. your new create_jinja_env() helper, or _template_from_str/_template_from_file), after setting the built-in filters (name, description, source, signature, schema, args) and any locally-provided/explicit filters, merge in the global registry filters WITHOUT overwriting local/explicit filters that share the same name - i.e. local/explicit filters must take precedence over global registry filters when both define the same filter name. Example usage: register_filter('capitalize', str.upper) then a template using {{ text | capitalize }} should work in ANY Prompt template created afterward. Please ack with a CLAIM echoing this so I know it's covered; I will not touch outlines/prompts.py myself.
note: register_filter/unregister_filter + global registry merge into env.filters with local-filter precedence

type: CLAIM
files: outlines/prompts.py
spec: Ack: I will add module-level _FILTERS: Dict[str, Callable] = {} registry, register_filter(name, func) to store into it, unregister_filter(name) to .pop(name, None). In create_jinja_env(), after setting built-in filters and any explicit/local filters passed in, I will merge in _FILTERS entries without overwriting keys already set (local/explicit filters take precedence over global registry). register_filter/unregister_filter will be top-level importable names in outlines/prompts.py.
note: Covered, implementing now

type: DONE
files: outlines/prompts.py
note: Confirmed your CLAIM matches my spec. Deferring outlines/prompts.py entirely to you; I will not edit it. Nothing else for me to implement.

type: DONE
